title: cleistes pluriflora
intro: Cleistes pluriflora, also known as the Many-flowered Cleistes, is a species of orchid native to South America, characterized by its bright yellow or greenish-yellow flowers and slender stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its purported medicinal properties, which are believed to include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Cleistes pluriflora include potential uses in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. Herbal preparations of Cleistes pluriflora are typically made by infusing the roots or leaves in water or other solvent to create a decoction or tincture, although more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of these preparations.
